mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are non-negative in >55% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp, or t-statistic for return difference is < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, expanding industrial production indicates successful capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth yields negative or flat 60-day returns during identified recovery regimes, or regime-conditional IC drops below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate economic activity and corporate earnings potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth correlates with negative 20-day forward returns in >50% of observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 200, inflationary pressures force monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, or threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion phases, declining housing starts signal peak residential investment and impending demand contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines during late-cycle expansions produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of instances, or regime-specific predictive power is indistinguishable from zero.
